target_after_day_90.communication_goal: Smooth everyday communication with locals across common daily-life, social, service, travel, housing, health, work, and opinion topics, excluding difficult technical, academic, political, literary, and rare vocabulary.

approximate_level.actfl: Intermediate Mid to Intermediate High behavior in familiar domains
approximate_level.cefr_equivalent_rough: A2+ to early B1 conversational behavior

must_be_able_to: handle multi-turn conversations in familiar situations
describe past, present, and near-future events
express needs, preferences, comparisons, reasons, and simple opinions
understand short natural dialogues with some repeated or slowed input
read practical Traditional Chinese text such as menus, signs, messages, receipts, simple notices, and chat messages
write short messages, requests, self-introductions, and practical notes

not_acceptable_if: each day only introduces a handful of words
grammar is presented only as formulas
sentence banks contain words absent from vocab
listening is just isolated sentence repetition
speaking drills only ask the learner to mimic, not respond
reviews are passive rather than production-based
acceptable_if: every lesson uses a closed-vocabulary validator
every new grammar point includes usage, non-usage, contrast, learner mistakes, and transformation drills
every day includes both controlled drills and open-response speaking
Days 31-90 shift from sentence learning to scenario handling

purpose: basic identity, family, time, location, food, shopping, directions, numbers, daily actions
first 700 to 1000 lexical items if intensive
day_1_to_30_role.limitation: Not enough for smooth communication unless Days 31-90 aggressively train recombination and scenario fluency.

phases.title: Survival Fluency Expansion
phases.primary_goal: Move from isolated sentence production to functional multi-turn exchanges.
communication_targets: handle restaurants, cafés, convenience stores, and markets
talk about time, schedule, and availability
pronunciation_focus: tone sandhi in 你好 / 很好 / 想買
neutral tone in common function words

phases.title: Narration and Social Life
phases.primary_goal: Enable the learner to describe experiences, make plans, and participate in casual social exchanges.
communication_targets: talk about yesterday, today, and tomorrow
grammar_focus: 正在 / 在 for ongoing action
會 for learned ability and future likelihood

phases.title: Transactions and Problem Solving
phases.primary_goal: Train the learner to solve practical problems in Taiwan using Mandarin.
grammar_focus: 應該 / 需要 / 必須
direction complements: 來 / 去 / 上 / 下 / 進 / 出
pronunciation_focus: reduced neutral tones in natural speech
common Taiwan reductions and casual particles

phases.title: Independent Daily Communication
phases.primary_goal: Make the learner capable of handling most non-technical everyday conversations with locals.
communication_targets: talk about work or study in non-technical terms
summarize a short message or conversation

day_31_90_framework.scenario: asking for something at a café or shop
buying items, asking about bags, receipts, payment
finding bathroom, elevator, exit, counter, classroom
explaining why you want or do not want something
talking about likes, dislikes, and choices
asking size, color, price, trying on
small talk about weather and feelings
talking about what you did yesterday
talking about things you have done before
talking about what you can do and are learning
making, accepting, declining, and changing plans
talking to landlord or repair person
asking for help after a problem
what should or must be done
if this happens, then do that
although something is true, another thing is also true
movement in and out, up and down
bringing, taking, coming back, going back
telling stories with interruption and questions
calling a shop, clinic, or friend
short chat messages in Traditional Chinese
where you are from, why you are learning, what you do
meeting a local and chatting for several minutes
arrive at a new place, ask directions, buy something, make small talk
health or housing problem from start to resolution
social plan with conflict and rescheduling
90-day final speaking, listening, reading, and writing checkpoint

minimum_required_sections.core_function: What communication task this grammar performs.
minimum_required_sections.when_not_to_use: At least 3 invalid or unnatural contexts.
minimum_required_sections.word_order_logic: Why the parts appear in this order.
minimum_required_sections.contrast_with_known_grammar: Compare against at least 1 older structure.
minimum_required_sections.english_contrast: Explain why direct English mapping may fail.
minimum_required_sections.correct_examples: At least 8 examples using only known vocab.
minimum_required_sections.incorrect_examples: At least 5 examples with clear correction.
minimum_required_sections.error_feedback_map: Specific feedback for predictable learner mistakes.

validation_steps.name: tokenize_traditional_text
validation_steps.description: Tokenize every Traditional Chinese string into known lexical units.

validation_steps.name: resolve_token_to_vocab_id
validation_steps.description: Every token must map to exactly one vocab_id.

validation_steps.name: reject_unknown_token
validation_steps.description: If a token is absent from vocab_master.json, fail the build.

validation_steps.name: reject_future_vocab
validation_steps.description: If a token belongs to a future day, fail the lesson.

validation_steps.name: validate_grammar_reference
validation_steps.description: Every sentence grammar_id must exist in grammar_master.json.

validation_steps.name: validate_audio_reference
validation_steps.description: Every audio_id must exist in audio_manifest.json.

error_format.suggested_action: Add token to same-day vocab or replace sentence.

tasks.name: Service Interaction
tasks.scenario: Order food, customize the order, ask price, respond to one clarification question.

tasks.name: Problem Solving
tasks.scenario: Explain a housing, transport, or shopping problem and ask for help.

tasks.name: Social Conversation
tasks.scenario: Talk with a local about background, hobbies, Taiwan life, and plans.

tasks.name: Natural Dialogue
tasks.scenario: Understand a short natural-speed conversation about plans or a practical problem.

tasks.name: Practical Reading
tasks.scenario: Read a menu, sign, short message, or notice.

tasks.name: Short Message
tasks.scenario: Write a short Traditional Chinese message to ask, explain, apologize, or confirm.

can_pass_if: uses known vocabulary without excessive English fallback
is understandable to a patient Taiwan Mandarin speaker

codex_instruction:
Build Days 31–90 of the Traditional Mandarin 90-day intensive course using the provided framework.

Do not create light app-style lessons. Each day is a complete intensive learning unit.

The learner has completed Days 1–30 and has a closed vocabulary, grammar, sentence, pronunciation, listening, and writing database. Days 31–90 must extend that database without violating closed-vocabulary dependency rules.

Every sentence, dialogue, exercise, grammar example, listening script, speaking prompt, and audio item must use only:
1. vocabulary introduced on prior days, or
2. vocabulary introduced in the same day.

No future vocabulary may appear.

Every grammar point must include:
- function
- pattern
- meaning
- real usage
- when to use
- when not to use
- word order logic
- contrast with prior grammar
- English contrast
- Korean contrast
- Taiwan usage notes
- correct examples
- incorrect examples
- transformation drills
- open production drills
- error feedback map

Days 31–45 focus on survival fluency expansion.
Days 46–60 focus on narration and social conversation.
Days 61–75 focus on real-world transactions and problem solving.
Days 76–90 focus on independent daily communication.

The final Day 90 target is not native fluency. It is practical, smooth everyday communication in Taiwan across common non-technical domains.

Implement validators before rendering lessons.
If a lesson contains unknown tokens, missing grammar IDs, missing audio IDs, or future vocabulary, fail the build.
